what_it_does: This file is a living noticeboard guide that documents a built-but-dormant operator-session token mechanism: installOperatorSession() mints a token via GET /api/operator-session and installs a window.fetch interceptor that adds an X-Operator-Session header to same-origin /api requests, so the server can distinguish a supervised operator session from an autonomous agent without trusting a client-side flag.
observations: Status is built + banked, with enforcement purpose (A) held indefinitely per Tim
The security principle is server-decides-never-client-flag because client flags are spoofable
The interceptor was verified structurally after a false-negative probe-instance read null was identified as measurement contamination
The only intended consumer is the L5 accept mechanism, which is unverified and held
References commits 558e4a8, 71246d1, and e3b9c84 and source file src/lib/operatorSession.ts
It links to board://item-78c63045 and mentions [[L5 propose/accept]]
The item is type guide, state living, author_session projection, channel empty
standouts: Enforcement is intentionally held indefinitely as an explicit decision by Tim
The mechanism exists but is inert by design, honestly labeled as foundation rather than working gate
It candidly states there is no live consumer and therefore no real verification of enforcement correctness
conventions: Follows frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, timestamps, and history
Uses structured sections: STATUS, LANE, ABOUT, KIND, SUMMARY, CLAIMS/DECISIONS, RELATIONS, OPEN_QUESTIONS/GAPS
Cites commits and source file paths as evidence
Uses bracketed verification/decision tags like [verified, structural] and [decision]
References another board item via both YAML links and inline [[...]] notation
concerns: No actual code or validation logic is shown in the file; claims rely on external commits
The window.fetch interceptor could be bypassed if requests are made through non-fetch paths
The L5 accept consumer is referenced but not defined here, leaving the end-to-end claim unverifiable from this file alone
The operational meaning of banked/dormant and the conditions for reactivating enforcement are not specified
notes: A living guide recording a built-but-dormant operator-session token interceptor, kept as a foundation while its L5-accept enforcement gate is intentionally held per Tim.
questions: What server-side context determines whether a session is supervised or autonomous?
How is the token validated on the server, and where is that logic?
What is L5 propose/accept and where is it documented?
What was the false-negative probe-instance and how was the measurement contamination resolved?
Under what conditions would the held enforcement be reactivated?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the stated purpose is to document the operator-session token mechanism and its held enforcement, and the file does exactly that while also noting the gap that no consumer currently enforces it.
apparent_themes: Enforcement gating by explicit human decision
intention_signals: Preserve the design rationale and implementation foundation for a future supervised-vs-autonomous gating mechanism, while transparently recording that enforcement is paused and untested.
novelty: Explicit banked status for a security mechanism built but deliberately inactive, with a named decision-maker (Tim), a self-documented measurement-contamination catch, and a candid statement that there is no live consumer.
contribution: Records the existence, rationale, dormancy, and commit-level evidence of a server-decided operator-session token mechanism, including an honest audit of its untested enforcement gap, which a fused system would keep as historical design context.
summary_for_embedding: A living noticeboard guide documenting a built-but-dormant server-decided operator-session token interceptor that adds an X-Operator-Session header to same-origin API requests, with its L5-accept enforcement intentionally held per Tim and no live consumer currently verifying the gate.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve the design rationale, implementation status, and deliberate enforcement hold of an operator-session discriminator for future supervised-vs-autonomous gating.
